text: The surgeon sutures the rectal mucosa and submucosa with 2-0 Vicryl, placing the mucosal knots on the luminal side. The rectal mucosa is examined for completeness of repair. Sharp dissection is used to separate the vagina and rectal muscle coats from the perineal body, facilitating closure of the rectal muscle layers. The rectal mucosa is visible protruding during digital examination. The internal sphincter is repaired in an end-to-end fashion using 2-0 Vicryl.

The surgeon identifies and mobilizes the retracted ends of the external sphincter, preparing for a double-breasted repair. Alice forceps are used to apply traction to the sphincter ends, while a digit inside the rectum confirms appropriate pressure during the crossover squeeze test. The anal verge is closed to prevent tension on the skin post-repair. The external anal sphincter is repaired using a double-breasted, mayo-type technique with three OPDS sutures placed in a mattress fashion, ensuring robust repair and improved early continence.

The surgeon places the final PDS suture, completing the double-breasted closure of the external sphincter. The pelvic floor muscles, including bulbocavernosus and pre-rectal fibres of puborectalis, are repaired using two ovicle sutures starting from the apex. The transversus peroneus muscle ends are sutured with two O-vicryl, reducing the defect size as the peroneum is closed. The superficial peroneal muscle is identified and closed for additional bulk to the peroneal body. The vaginal repair begins with the deep layer using two ovicular sutures, except for the external anal sphincter, which uses three OPDS. The skin and vaginal mucosa are sutured with three ovicular repeat sutures. The layers of the vagina are examined to reconstruct the introitus for a good cosmetic outcome, and the vaginal mucosal injury is completely repaired with three ovicular repeat sutures.

The surgeon places subcutaneous sutures in the perianal and perineal body areas, followed by subcutaneous tuovical sutures to repair the labia. Three ovicral repeat sutures are used for the skin, completing the reconstruction of the introitus and bringing the skin of the perineum together. A digital examination confirms the absence of a rectovaginal fistula and the integrity of the repair.
